Q1
2020
Oceanside
Oceanside’s receipts from Janu­
ary through March were 16.6% be­
low the first sales period in 2019 but 
this decline was exaggerated by the 
Governor’s recent Executive Order 
allowing some businesses an extra 
90-days to file their tax return amidst 
the pandemic. Actual sales were 
down an estimated 2.9%.
Receipts from local casual din­
ing restaurants declined after the 
State’s lockdown on March 19 that 
closed dining rooms at the tail-end 
of the quarter. 
Auto-transportation related sales 
were lower as consumers grew con­
servative at the beginning of the 
recession, which officially began 
in February. Receipts at electron­
ics-appliance stores and most oth­
